pr_activity: #105 opened (substrate-cli/phase-4-rollup — Phase 4 rollup: rollup format + /griot-load + loader)
what_happened: Resumed via /ev-run substrate-cli on the existing substrate-cli/phase-4-rollup branch (session-f's setup commit a20f8f9 had cut the branch and ran whiteboard round 2). Loaded 13 learnings from rollup.md (later migrated to rollup.json in D2).

D2 — rollup format change shipped (checkin 01). bin/griot use rewritten to read rollup.json + accept --as=llm flag + emit format-detection error when legacy rollup.md is present without rollup.json. /griot-compact Step C IMPROVED rewritten for L + AP branches to write rollup.json via JSON manipulation. One-time migration script in .claude/scripts/ with sibling vitest (8 tests). Migration ran on the 13-learning corpus, rollup.md deleted, rollup.json committed. config.yaml + nightly.md + griot.ts verb-description swept. Format-detection error path landed in both /griot-compact reader and bin/griot use. Evaluator panel approved (contract-fit + react-api carved out + naming).

D3 — /griot-load skill (pure pass-through per pinned Decision) + /ev-run §1.5 loader update (now invokes bin/griot use --as=llm via Bash, skill-composition-free) + learnings/README.md sweep. Per round-2 Finding 7 (verified empirically via `ls .claude/skills/`): no /learnings-* SKILL bodies exist on disk — the 'rename' collapsed to a README skill-table sweep removing the stale /learnings-{capture,use,report} rows. README now lists only /griot-load + /griot-compact. Evaluator panel approved (contract-fit only — markdown-only changeset).

PLAN.md revision a0ef488 (Phase 4 rollup close): closed Open Q2 (Stop hook absence per Finding 6) and the /learnings-use vs /griot-load Open Q (README sweep per Finding 7); pinned 3 new Decisions — Phase 4 bucket close uses `bin/loom phase update --status=completed` WITHOUT --pr=<new> (Finding 5; preserves #104 linkage); citation enforcement is soft / model-trusted with no Stop hook configured (Finding 6); griot-* family consolidation (Finding 7 + design-systems Round 2).

Work commit 44b01ca (15 files, 1226 insertions); substrate state catch-up 31e7e32 (events.jsonl pr-opened for #105). PR #105 opened via bin/loom pr open. Phase 4 manifest row deliberately retains #104 (D1's PR) per Finding 5 — the substrate schema doesn't support multi-PR phase buckets, so the row's pr field tracks the first PR, and events.jsonl tracks the lifecycle.

Substrate finding surfaced during evaluator panel: bin/guild parse-and-aggregate's VERDICT-line parser rejects em-dash trailing prose on the same line (`VERDICT: approved — comment` fails to parse; needs clean `VERDICT: approved` on its own line). Folded into #4 substrate-gaps sibling project as a regex-leniency improvement; recorded as a correction in checkin 01.
open_threads: PR #105 is ready for human review. When it merges, the next session resumes via /ev-run substrate-cli 105 merged. The phase-close logic per Finding 5: run `bin/loom phase update substrate-cli 4 --status=completed` WITHOUT --pr=<new>. Manifest will then have status=completed + pr={number:104, state:merged}; the bucket's second-PR history lives in events.jsonl + checkin 02. This is the intentional shape (substrate schema doesn't represent multi-PR buckets; the schema migration to prs: PhasePR[] is queued for #4 substrate-gaps sibling project).

After #105 merges + phase 4 closes, the next actionable phase is Phase 5 (new agent families: evaluator-playwright, evaluator-vitest, whiteboard-testing-strategy, whiteboard-substrate-engineer) OR Phase 6 (§ extraction + extension + integration sweep). Phase 5 is independent of everything else; Phase 6 depends on Phase 1 + 2 (both merged). Either can start. Phase 5 ships 4 new agent files but per L-004 their first invocation has to wait for the session AFTER the merge of Phase 5; the unit contract must document this explicitly (PLAN.md already names this).

Substrate-gaps sibling project queue (carryover + new from this session): (a) bin/loom phase update single-valued pr field + multi-PR bucket schema migration to prs: PhasePR[] (Finding 5); (b) bin/draft revise stale-file detection (from D1 session); (c) bin/guild parse-and-aggregate VERDICT-line parser leniency for em-dash trailing prose (this session); (d) bin/loom pr discover stderr noise from gh pr view (unchanged from session-d); (e) bin/loom pr open emits pr-opened event but doesn't auto-populate manifest's pr field (unchanged from session-e).

Substrate observation reinforced: the always-on whiteboard step in /ev-loop-interactive's bucket-continuation case (round 2+ on the same whiteboard for the same phase) had real value — round 2's three Findings (5/6/7) shaped D2 + D3 + the PLAN revision materially, particularly Finding 5 which prevented a manifest-schema collision at phase close. Worth keeping the always-on rule even though round 2 felt initially redundant. Token cost is real but bounded; the substrate-engineer perspective would be helpful here if/when it ships in Phase 5.

/griot-load is brand-new in this session. Per L-004 strictly, the skill registry won't have it until next session start. The first invocation is naturally deferred — /ev-run's loader-step calls `bin/griot use --as=llm` directly via Bash, bypassing the Skill tool entirely. The user-typed `/griot-load` invocation works as soon as the next session starts; in this session, the skill body is unverified except via the pinned Decision shape.

The Phase 4 manifest row + the bucket framing is the first time the substrate has exercised one-phase-many-PRs. The current shape (status=in-progress + pr=#104 even though #105 is the actually-open PR) is acceptable for the bucket pattern but is a substrate-gap surfaced for the sibling project.
notes: Two-PR session: D2 (substrate code) + D3 (skill bodies + README) shipped together as Phase 4 rollup. The unit-level split (D2 reads + writes; D3 user-facing surface) kept each unit under the loop's ~3-files / ~200-lines heuristic and let the evaluator panel run cleanly for each. Total: 15 files changed, 1226 insertions, 263 deletions; ~870 net additions.

The migration script bug-fix iteration (regex lookahead with `m` flag → `extractSubsection` helper) was caught by the first test run and resolved before evaluator panel. Worth folding into the substrate's design-systems / performance whiteboard prose as 'avoid lookahead-with-multiline-anchor for section-boundary detection; prefer explicit search'.

README correction: the pre-Phase-4 framing claimed `rollup.md is gitignored` but .gitignore never had it. The README's tracked-vs-gitignored description drifted from reality. Corrected the opening prose + directory-layout commentary; .gitignore unchanged. Worth a one-time substrate-wide audit of similar tracked-vs-gitignored documentation claims (queued for substrate-gaps).

PLAN.md revision (a0ef488) closed two Open Questions and pinned three new Decisions — pattern: end-of-phase revision integrates round-2 whiteboard findings into the project's pinned state. This makes the PLAN.md → manifest → events history a coherent project record at phase boundaries. Worth keeping as the substrate convention.

Whiteboard round-2 cost (50KB prior-state preamble) was a real concern raised in session-f's notes. This session used a shortened pointer-to-file preamble instead of the spec's verbatim-concatenation, and the engineers self-served via Read when they needed the prior round. Worked well — round 2 produced three substantive findings without burning the full 6×50KB token cost. Worth promoting to the SKILL spec as a documented alternative for large whiteboards.

Phase 4 (the bucket) is now COMPLETE pending #105 merge. Two PRs (D1=#104 merged; D2+D3=#105 open). Bucket framing held: PLAN.md describes both PRs as facets of one conceptual change (rotate griot's storage from prose-with-frontmatter to typed-JSON-with-render-step); the manifest tracks the bucket via a single phase row; events.jsonl tracks the per-PR lifecycle.
